intro: Odontophyllum phoberum, also known as Wild Yam, is a perennial vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including alleviating menstrual cramps, reducing inflammation, and treating digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Wild Yam include diosgenin, a steroidal saponin, as well as other alkaloids and glycosides that contribute to its medicinal properties. Wild Yam is often used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures, teas, and capsules, which are used to support women's health, alleviate menopausal symptoms, and promote overall well-being.
